mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Pace S or P400 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ126 engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only; a reconditioned AJ20P6 costs £5,000–£8,500. Used units are available from £3,000–£5,000. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. One owner reported a shocking £23,000 bill for an engine replacement on a low-mileage F-Pace — highlighting the importance of warranty coverage.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Pace S / P400 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. The AJ126 (2016–2020) has well-documented weaknesses: plastic coolant pipes that crack catastrophically and timing chain tensioner wear. The AJ20P6 (2021+) Ingenium straight-six is more modern with promising early reliability, but the complex 48V mild-hybrid system is unproven long-term. Both demand diligent maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Pace S and P400?

acceptedAnswer.text: AJ126: plastic coolant pipe failure (catastrophic — upgrade to aluminium pipes), timing chain tensioner wear (at 60,000–90,000 miles), supercharger coupler wear, oil leaks. AJ20P6: mild-hybrid system complexity, potential oil filter housing issues (subject to recall), and general Ingenium petrol engine maintenance. The AJ126's cooling system is the single biggest concern.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Pace S / P400 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: F-Pace values remain strong — a well-kept example is often worth £18,000–£40,000+. A £4,500–£8,500 reconditioned engine is 15–35% of the vehicle's value — within the Jaguar 60% scrap threshold. If the car is a cherished example with good bodywork and interior, replacement is often worthwhile. However, given the £23,000 replacement bill reported, ensure you understand the full cost before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Pace S or P400 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. Pre-facelift (2016–2020) uses AJ126; facelift (2021+) uses AJ20P6. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ126 plastic coolant pipes, or do they need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: They need replacement — the plastic pipes crack and cannot be reliably repaired. Replacement with upgraded aluminium pipes costs £600–£1,500 and is highly recommended. If the pipes fail and cause overheating, the engine can be destroyed. At the first sign of coolant loss or bubbling coolant, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar F-Pace S / P400 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current F-Pace S/P400 market. AJ126-engined cars sit at the lower end of the range; AJ20P6 Ingenium straight-six examples command a premium. One owner reported a £23,000 engine replacement bill on a low-mileage F-Pace — highlighting the importance of warranty coverage on these complex engines.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an F-Pace S/P400 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The V6's supercharger and the inline-six's mild-hybrid system add complexity.

problems.title: Plastic Coolant Pipe Failure (AJ126 — Pre-Facelift)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, rising temperature gauge, steam from the engine bay, overheating, bubbling coolant, warning light

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: a cracked plastic coolant pipe can dump coolant rapidly, leading to catastrophic engine failure

problems.recommendation: The AJ126's cooling system is its single biggest weakness. The plastic crossover pipes under the supercharger become brittle with repeated heat cycles and can crack catastrophically. One owner reported: "It began with overheating, which I later traced to the cracked plastic V pipe under the supercharger—apparently a well-known weak spot on these engines that often fails catastrophically". Upgraded aluminium pipes are available on eBay and are highly recommended. Check coolant levels regularly. If you see bubbling coolant, stop driving immediately — the engine may already be damaged.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner and Guide Wear (AJ126)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the front or top of the engine, engine warning light, cam timing errors, eventual chain slip
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (timing chain, tensioner, guide and sprocket replacement)
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£7,500 (if chain jumps and causes valve interference)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the timing chain fails on this interference engine, pistons hit valves and the engine is destroyed

problems.recommendation: The AJ126 shares the same timing chain tensioner issues as the larger AJ133 V8, though they tend to occur at higher mileages. The timing chain's service life is typically 100,000–150,000 km (62,000–93,000 miles). If you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately. A preventative tensioner and guide replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes with the correct 5W-20 specification are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Supercharger Coupler Wear (AJ126)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the supercharger at idle, metallic tick that changes with engine speed, reduced power delivery
problems.recommendation: The supercharger coupler/torsion isolator is a known wear item on both the AJ126 and AJ133 engines. It can become "very rattly" at higher mileages. If you hear a rattling noise from the engine at idle that changes with revs, have the supercharger coupler inspected. Replacement is a specialist job but far cheaper than a full supercharger rebuild.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: F-Pace S / P400 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Upgrade to aluminium pipes — cheaper than a new engine
Labour-intensive but far cheaper than a full engine replacement
If chain jumps and valves hit pistons, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This F-Pace S / P400? Check These First
buyIf: * Facelift model (2021+) with the AJ20P6 Ingenium straight-six — the more modern and efficient engine with promising reliability
* AJ126-engined car with documented coolant pipe upgrade (aluminium pipes fitted) — the defining weakness addressed
* Evidence of timing chain inspection or replacement on AJ126 cars over 60,000 miles
* Full service history with correct JAG-spec oil (5W-20 for AJ126, 0W-20 for AJ20P6)
* Car still under manufacturer warranty — these are complex, expensive engines to repair
avoidIf: * AJ126 with no proof of coolant pipe work — plastic pipes will fail
* AJ126 with any cold-start rattle — timing chain wear is likely
* AJ126 with evidence of repeated coolant top-ups — the cooling system is failing
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Any engine warning light without a clear diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The F-Pace S and P400 are powered by two very different six-cylinder engines. The AJ126 (2016–2020) is a supercharged V6 with a well-documented Achilles' heel — plastic coolant pipes that fail catastrophically, and timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileages. The AJ20P6 (2021+) is a modern straight-six with mild-hybrid technology — early reliability is promising, but the complex 48V system is unproven long-term. Buy on history, maintain religiously, and keep it under warranty. Skip the history and it will punish you.

cta.label: → Compare F-Pace S / P400 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Pace S or P400 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (AJ126 plastic coolant pipe warning — the most critical check on the pre-facelift car)
2. Cold Start — Any rattling? (AJ126 timing chain tensioner warning)
3. Supercharger Noise — Any rattling at idle? (AJ126 coupler wear warning)
4. Service History — including which generation (AJ126 or AJ20P6) and evidence of major maintenance
5. Oil Leaks — Any oil under the car? (AJ126 oil leaks or AJ20P6 oil filter housing recall)
6. MHEV Health (AJ20P6) — Any warning lights or reduced performance? (48V system issues)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst, O2 sensors)
